title: Missing nonReentrant guard on updateBorrowLedger allows reentrancy attack
reason: Rejected. `updateBorrowLedger` is internal-only, and every reachable caller in this codebase (`redeemInternal` and `repayBorrowInternal`) already runs under the contract-level `nonReentrant` guard.

title: Admin can set malicious Compound and Curve addresses to steal user funds
reason: Rejected. This is an admin-trust/governance-risk observation, not a standalone code vulnerability under the stated reporting standard.

title: No minimum amount validation on Compound withdrawals
reason: Rejected as a standalone item. The reportable issue is the specific redeem path that ignores Compound withdrawal failure and still burns full shares; the generic 'no minimum' framing is too broad.

title: No validation of external protocol return values
reason: Rejected as a standalone item. The claim is overbroad; most of the cited calls are not independently exploitable, while the materially harmful unchecked-return cases are captured in the specific findings above.

title: Unchecked return value from SafeERC20.approve
reason: Rejected. `safeApprove` does not return an unchecked boolean here; it reverts on low-level failure or on a decoded `false` return.

title: No two-step admin transfer or timelock on critical functions
reason: Rejected. Lack of timelock is governance hardening, not a concrete protocol vulnerability in this threat model; admin transfer itself already uses a pending-admin acceptance flow.

title: Division precision loss in interest calculations
reason: Rejected. Integer truncation is expected in fixed-point accounting and no concrete exploit path or material protocol harm was demonstrated.

title: Redemption can return less than requested without user notification
reason: Rejected as a standalone item because the material issue is stronger and already captured: the contract can burn the full share amount while paying only the reduced cash amount.

title: Exchange rate calculation excludes reserves in certain conditions
reason: Rejected. The cited implementation already subtracts reserves via `getCashExReserves()` before computing `(cash + borrows) / totalSupply`; the candidate does not show a real edge-case bug.
